'''David F. Bischoff''' ([[December 15]], [[1950s#1951|1951]]–[[March 19]], [[2018]]) was an [[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] science fiction writer and author of "[[Be Still My Heart: The Bartender's Tale]]," a short story in ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''.

==Biography==
Born in Washington D.C. before living in [[Wikipedia:Eugene, Oregon|Eugene]], Oregon, David Bischoff was the author of various science fiction series including the ''Gaming Magi'' series and the ''Dragonstar Trilogy'' with [[Wikipedia:Thomas Monteleone|Thomas Monteleone]]. He also wrote novelizations for [[Wikipedia:WarGames|''WarGames'']], [[Wikipedia:The Blob (1988 film)|''The Blob'']], and [[Wikipedia:Gremlins 2: The New Batch|''Gremlins 2: The New Batch'']].

He also worked on various television series such as [[Wikipedia:Star Trek: The Next Generation|''Star Trek: The Next Generation'']], where he wrote the episodes ''[[Wikipedia:Tin Man (Star Trek: The Next Generation)|Tin Man]]'' and ''[[Wikipedia:First Contact (Star Trek: The Next Generation)|First Contact]]'' (not to be confused with the ''Star Trek'' movie ''[[Wikipedia:Star Trek: First Contact|First Contact]]''). He also wrote the ''Star Trek'' tie-in novel ''[[w:c:memory-alpha:Grounded (novel)|Grounded]],'' which spent time on the bestseller list.

Other TV work included ''Dinosaucers'' produced by [[Wikipedia:DIC Entertainment|DIC Entertainment]]. His interest in dinosaurs led him to write the second of 24 books in the [[Wikipedia:Time Machine (book series)|''Time Machine'']] series, ''Search for Dinosaurs''.

In addition to some seventy-five original novels, Bischoff also wrote tie-in novels for series such as [[Wikipedia:Farscape|''Farscape'']], [[Wikipedia:Space Precinct|''Space Precinct'']], and [[Wikipedia:Jonny Quest|''Jonny Quest'']], a number of them under pseudonyms. He also wrote show-business related nonfiction under a variety of names.
